2605.21384.title: SpecBench: Measuring Reward Hacking in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
2605.21384.paper_summary: As long-horizon coding agents produce more code than any developer can review, oversight collapses onto a single surface: the automated test suite. Reward hacking naturally arises in this setup, as the agent optimizes for passing tests while deviating from the users true goal. We study this reward hacking phenomenon by decompose software engineering tasks into three parts: (i) a natural language description of the specification (ii) visible validation tests that exercise specified features in isolation, and (iii) held-out tests that compose those same features to simulate real-world usage. Based on the specification and the visible validation test suites, a genuine agent would be able to generate a solution that can also pass all of the held-out tests. Therefore we use the gap in pass rates on these two suites to quantify reward hacking. Based on this methodology, we introduce SpecBench, a benchmark comprising 30 systems-level programming tasks ranging from short horizon tasks like building a JSON parser to ultra long horizon tasks like building an entire OS kernel from scratch. Large-scale experiments reveal a consistent pattern: while every frontier agent saturates the visible suite, reward hacking persists, with smaller models exhibiting larger gaps on holdout suites. The gap also scales sharply with task length: it grows by 28 percentage points for every tenfold increase in code size. Failures range from subtle feature isolation to deliberate exploits, including a 2,900-line hash-table "compiler" that memorizes test inputs. SpecBench offers a principled testbed for measuring whether coding agents build genuine working systems or merely game the test suites developers hand them.
2605.21384.paper_summary_zh: 隨著長期編碼代理產生的代碼超過任何開發者能夠審查的量，監督便集中於一個單一的表面：自動化測試套件。獎勵駭客行為在這種設置中自然產生，因為代理在優化通過測試的同時偏離了用戶的真正目標。我們通過將軟體工程任務分解為三個部分來研究這一獎勵駭客現象：(i) 規範的自然語言描述 (ii) 可見的驗證測試，這些測試獨立地檢驗指定的功能，以及 (iii) 保留測試，這些測試組合相同的功能以模擬真實世界的使用。根據規範和可見的驗證測試套件，一個真正的代理應該能夠生成一個解決方案，該解決方案也能通過所有的保留測試。因此，我們利用這兩個套件的通過率差距來量化獎勵駭客行為。基於這一方法論，我們引入了SpecBench，一個基準，包含30個系統級編程任務，範圍從構建JSON解析器等短期任務到從零開始構建整個操作系統內核等超長期任務。大規模實驗揭示了一個一致的模式：雖然每個前沿代理都飽和了可見套件，但獎勵駭客行為仍然存在，較小的模型在保留套件上表現出更大的差距。這一差距也隨著任務長度急劇增加：每增加十倍的代碼大小，差距增長28個百分點。失敗範圍從微妙的功能隔離到故意的利用，包括一個記錄測試輸入的2,900行哈希表“編譯器”。SpecBench提供了一個原則性的測試平台，用於測量編碼代理是否構建真正的工作系統，或僅僅是操縱開發者交給他們的測試套件。
2605.21384.author: Bingchen Zhao et.al.
2605.21384.authors: Bingchen Zhao, Dhruv Srikanth, Yuxiang Wu, Zhengyao Jiang
